Like they've been here for three, four weeks.
They're probably four or five weeks.
They were in the previous class.
And so one, they didn't go through proper onboarding and then two, they're just giving her a lot.
Like she's just having to edit.

Like, you know, it says 2023 or something like things like that, where it's like, and I think she thinks that it's, they're just piling it on to because they're like struggling with the volume, but they're not really using the workflows, which the point of the workflows is so we can like 10x the volume, but not 10x the work for anybody.
Right.
So anyway, there's just a, almost like a lot.
What's the saying?
Like death by a paper cuts.
Like no one big problem, but there's like lots of little things that if you pile them up, it's like, oh, someone's just writing on their own and not using any of the tooling.
So, I don't know.
Which is fine for delivery maybe, but like does nothing for long-term protocols, I guess, right?
Yeah, exactly.
That's the, that's the struggle.
It's like, we do have 50 customers and we have to show them value ASAP.
So we're doing things.
Like in a non, a lot of things in a non scalable way, but you do need to strike the balance of like, I think it's like, it's like deciding, okay, show me exactly what broke and what was your issue.
Is that like a true problem that's going to take engineering time to fix?
And yes, so then just go right.
Or are you just like straight up doing it wrong?
Or you just prompted it terribly?
Something like that.
Like you kind of need, we kind of need to decide which ones are like people process issue versus like true product issue and then handle those differently.
So that's kind of how I think about it.
But I don't know.
Everyone's, everyone's new.
We're just trying to get the product out the door.
And we'll figure it out over time.
So like the more feedback everyone can share, the better.
Yeah, what else can I share with you?
No, think the workshop video will be helpful.
It's like
That's the three-hour one you mentioned, right?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Like the vision, to me at least, the vision is so crystal clear and like necessary and makes sense.
It's just turning that into, I mean, that's this problem with the struggle of all startups.
It's just like operationalizing that.
Like Marcel was at one point running all 20 first customer accounts himself, basically.
He can do that because he's like a machine and he built this and he knows exactly.
He's very good at prompting.
He is, he's super, obviously he's like CMO level.
He's like super strategic and has like very creative mind, right?
So he can do that.
So now we're trying to replicate that, but we have a whole org now and we have different roles and responsibilities.
So there's many different layers to that to make it successful.
And that's the whole bet we're making is that we can turn like Marcel's brain into a company, right?
So that's why I want you all to spend a lot of time with him.
And then just a really down.
Like how he thinks about the Oregon, you know, the customers and the use cases, how to show value and all of that.
And then the tech will follow.
just takes time for sure.
We will have a platform soon.
I would say by the end of the summer, there should be something that like the clients need to log into something.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
Pigeon hold as a service.
It's like they just wait for our updates.
They need to be able to see results, dashboards.
They need to be able to like see all the stuff we've done.
I think it's really easy to forget.
Yeah, if it's just piecemeal here and there, you're like, yeah.
But if you can see it all, yeah.
And I think the goal might be to, yeah, exactly.
We should be able to also potentially like sell individual pieces of the tech as well.
Like in our last board meeting, we were showing him, Karan, who's a board member.
Is he from Madrona?
Yeah, from Madrona.
with retail também.
Do I appreciate putting so talk learn
Showing him the competitor research one and the fact checker one.
Like we have all these different workflows that we've built.
And it's like.
So you can sell the modules separately.
Yeah, I think so.
Because he was like, oh, I mean, shouldn't like SDRs use this if it's doing like competitive research and producing a brief on that?
Yeah, there's lots of companies who are building an entire company just to do that.
Like we could just slice up a little module, make it available with an API or something for someone to use that.
So I think like the market is way beyond content marketing teams to definitely all of growth and GTM.
Like there's no reason why any of those tools are not useful for sales or customer success.
And then the bigger vision, which Marcel will go through, is just like all like just creating all of these workflows for knowledge workers in general.
Like even all of us, how we're going through our days, like what are we doing over and over again?
What is the process like Matthew, what's the process like the diff?
Between the original piece he received and then the edits and then like another version, another version.
Like we can learn from all of those.
Our recruiting workflows like we built.
So when you prompt it and it gives you a job description and then you ask it based on this job description, what questions should I ask?
It produces you questions for the application and for the video interview and then it can watch and read and grade them.
And it's not amazing.
Obviously, we spend no time trying to make that better.
Obviously, we're focused on content workflows, but like that could be a product itself.
So it's really it's all very similar, like at the foundation.
So just figuring out like different ways to package it up and where to focus is always the debate.
OK, anyway, that's enough for today from me.
Yeah, if you're stuck, there's a channel called D Onboarding.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Just throw onboarding questions in there.
You're stuck.
That way I don't have to be like bottleneck if you're like, I don't know how to find this tool.
I don't know how to figure out this process.
Like just start there.
And yeah, we can go from there.
Cool.
Cool.
Sounds good.
Awesome.
All right.
Talk to you all soon.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Onboard new directors Darrell and Megan, providing an overview of GrowthX's onboarding process, tools, and current challenges.](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Focus on company-wide onboarding tasks in Linear for the first 3-4 days](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Content Manager onboarding includes a 2-week bootcamp with writing simulations](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1141.0)
  - [GrowthX is rapidly scaling, with nascent workflows and tools still being developed](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1662.0)
  - [Feedback on tool/workflow issues is crucial for improving processes and technology](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)

## Topics

### Onboarding Process Overview

  - [Company-wide onboarding tasks in Linear project, estimated 3-4 days to complete](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=303.0)
  - [Content Manager 2-week bootcamp includes writing simulations and tool training](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1141.0)
  - [Marcel (CEO) to provide in-depth sessions on vision, strategy, and customer approach](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1420.0)
  - [Unlimited PTO policy; add time off to calendar as free all-day events](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1298.0)

### Tools and Workflows

  - [Linear used for project management and feedback to engineering](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=627.0)
  - [Notion houses company handbook and documentation](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=303.0)
  - [Custom workflows being built to replace Airtable, migration expected in 4 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1862.0)
  - [Some content managers bypassing tools due to frustrations, using ChatGPT instead](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1674.0)

### Current Challenges and Initiatives

  - [Rapid scaling leading to inconsistent onboarding and training](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1709.0)
  - [Need for better feedback systems on workflow/tool issues](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)
  - [Balancing immediate customer value delivery with building scalable processes](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=2027.0)
  - [Developing a client-facing platform, expected by end of summer](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=2188.0)

### Future Vision

  - [Potential to sell individual workflow modules (e.g., competitor research, fact-checker)](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=2205.0)
  - [Expanding beyond content marketing to general knowledge worker workflows](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=2263.0)
  - [Exploring applications in sales, customer success, and recruitment](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=2259.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Complete company-wide onboarding tasks in Linear](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Meet with Matthew to discuss role expectations and team dynamics](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1554.0)
  - [Watch Marcel's 3-hour workshop video for in-depth understanding of vision](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
  - [Attend upcoming director meetings and SF office happy hour (Megan)](https://fathom.video/share/FYm6rKBhDAC7hAQHh7U51r-vLi9HDvys?tab=summary&timestamp=1792.0)
  - Use \#d-onboarding Slack channel for questions and assistance

action_items.description: Complete company-wide onboarding tasks in Linear. This includes setting up accounts, adding photos, roles, and details.

Meet with Matthew today to discuss onboarding, roles, and responsibilities. Bridget mentioned that he might want you to go through the content manager onboarding process as well.
